primary_sources.title: Election Commission of Malaysia (SPR) — Official Results, 12th General Election, 8 March 2008
Statement of Results, 12th General Election (Dewan Rakyat seats and State Legislative Assemblies)
Loh, F.K.W. Old vs New Politics in Malaysia (SIRD, 2009) — GE12 analysis

notes: The 'political tsunami' of 2008. Barisan Nasional loses two-thirds parliamentary majority for the first time since 1969. Opposition (DAP + PKR + PAS, later formalised as Pakatan Rakyat) wins 82 federal seats and five state governments (Penang, Selangor, Perak, Kedah, Kelantan; Perak lost via defections 2009). The threshold critical juncture for P5-to-P6 transition. Causes documented: HINDRAF 2007 mobilisation, Bersih 1 (November 2007), Lingam tape, MyKad delisting issues, internet-era information flow. EC legitimacy delta is negative because the result revealed how much malapportionment had been protecting BN's seat share — BN won 51.4% popular vote but 63.1% of seats.
